653	\section{The functor of points}
654	\label{sec:grquot_functor}
655	
656	The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme \(T\) to the set of \emph{equivalence classes} of
657	rank-\(d\) quotients of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\). The following block packages a single such
658	quotient, the equivalence relation on them, and the pullback action through which the
659	functor acts on morphisms; the two coherence blocks after it isolate the comparison
660	identities that make that action functorial.
661	
662	\begin{definition}[Rank-\(d\) quotients and their pullback action]
663	  \label{def:gr_rankQuotient}
664	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient,
665	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.Rel,
666	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_refl,
667	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_symm,
668	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_trans,
669	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqSetoid,
670	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqPullback,
671	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqPullback_rel}
672	  \uses{lem:gr_pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
673	        def:is_locally_free_of_rank}
674	  A \emph{rank-\(d\) quotient of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)} on a scheme \(T\) is a triple
675	  \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) consisting of a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-modules
676	  \(\mathcal{F}\) that is locally free of rank \(d\) (\cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank})
677	  together with an epimorphism \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) out
678	  of the trivial rank-\(r\) bundle. Two such quotients \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\)
679	  and \(y = \langle \mathcal{F}', q'\rangle\) are \emph{equivalent}, written \(x \sim y\),
680	  when there is an isomorphism \(f : \mathcal{F} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{F}'\) with
681	  \(q \circ f = q'\) (equivalently \(\ker q = \ker q'\)). This relation is reflexive
682	  (\(f = \mathrm{id}\)), symmetric (\(f \mapsto f^{-1}\)), and transitive (composition of the
683	  witnessing isomorphisms), hence an equivalence relation -- the setoid whose quotient is the
684	  functor's value at \(T\).
685	
686	  For a morphism \(\psi : T' \to T\) the \emph{pullback action} sends
687	  \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) to
688	  \[
689	    \psi^{*}x \;=\;
690	      \bigl\langle\, \psi^{*}\mathcal{F},\;
691	        \mathcal{O}_{T'}^{\,r} \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
692	        \psi^{*}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\bigr) \xrightarrow{\ \psi^{*}q\ }
693	        \psi^{*}\mathcal{F} \,\bigr\rangle,
694	  \]
695	  re-presenting the source as the trivial bundle via the free-pullback isomorphism
696	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}. The result is again a rank-\(d\) quotient: the composite is
697	  an epimorphism because pullback, a left adjoint, preserves epimorphisms and the
698	  re-presentation is an isomorphism, and \(\psi^{*}\mathcal{F}\) is locally free of rank \(d\)
699	  by \cref{lem:gr_pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank}. The pullback action respects the equivalence
700	  relation -- given \(f\) witnessing \(x \sim y\), the pulled-back isomorphism \(\psi^{*}f\)
701	  witnesses \(\psi^{*}x \sim \psi^{*}y\), by functoriality of pullback -- so it descends to a
702	  map of equivalence classes.
703	\end{definition}
704	
705	The two blocks below record the comparison identities, between the free-pullback isomorphism
706	\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} and Mathlib's pseudofunctor structure on pullback, that make
707	the descended pullback action of \cref{def:gr_rankQuotient} functorial.
708	
709	\begin{lemma}\leanok
710	[Identity coherence of the free-pullback comparison]
711	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
712	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
713	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
714	  For the identity morphism \(\mathrm{id}_T\), the canonical comparison morphism of unit
715	  modules \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*}\mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}\) -- the map underlying the
716	  free-pullback isomorphism \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} at a one-element index -- coincides
717	  with the component at \(\mathbf{1}\) of the pullback-identity isomorphism
718	  \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*} \cong \mathrm{id}\).
719	\end{lemma}
720	
721	\begin{proof}
722	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
723	  The comparison morphism is the mate of \(\mathrm{id}_{\mathbf 1}\) under the
724	  pullback--pushforward adjunction: the image of the identity of \(\mathbf 1\) through the
725	  adjunction's hom-equivalence on the unit. For the identity scheme morphism the pushforward
726	  is the identity functor, so the adjunction unit is itself an identity; Mathlib's
727	  pullback-identity isomorphism is exactly the conjugate (left-adjoint transpose) of the
728	  pushforward-identity isomorphism. Tracing the identity through the hom-equivalence and the
729	  conjugate-transpose identity for the pullback-identity isomorphism shows the comparison
730	  equals the \(\mathbf 1\)-component of that isomorphism.
731	\end{proof}
732	
733	\begin{lemma}\leanok
734	[Identity coherence at the free sheaf]
735	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_id}
736	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_id}
737	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
738	  For the identity morphism \(\mathrm{id}_T\) and an index set \(I\), the free-pullback
739	  isomorphism \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*}(\bigoplus_I \mathbf 1) \cong \bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\) coincides
740	  with the component at the free sheaf \(\bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\) of the pullback-identity
741	  isomorphism \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*} \cong \mathrm{id}\).
742	\end{lemma}
743	
744	\begin{proof}
745	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
746	  Both sides are cocones on the coproduct \(\bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\); by coproduct
747	  extensionality it suffices to check equality after precomposing with each coprojection
748	  \(\mathbf 1 \to \bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\), which reduces to the one-element case
749	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}.
750	\end{proof}
751	
752	\begin{lemma}\leanok
753	[Composite coherence of the free-pullback comparison]
754	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
755	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
756	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id, def:modules_pullbackComp}
757	  For composable scheme morphisms \(a : T'' \to T'\) and \(b : T' \to T\), the comparison
758	  morphism of unit modules \((b \circ a)^{*}\mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}\) factors as
759	  \(a^{*}\) of the comparison for \(b\), the comparison for \(a\), and the component at
760	  \(\mathbf{1}\) of the iterated-pullback comparison \(a^{*}b^{*} \cong (b\circ a)^{*}\)
761	  (\cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}).
762	\end{lemma}
763	
764	\begin{proof}
765	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id, def:modules_pullbackComp}
766	  This is the composite analogue of \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}: the mate
767	  construction is compatible with composition of adjunctions, so the comparison for
768	  \(b \circ a\) is the pasting of the comparisons for \(a\) and \(b\) reassociated by the
769	  pseudofunctor comparison \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. The pentagon coherence of the
770	  iterated-pullback comparisons makes the factorisation well-defined.
771	\end{proof}
772	
773	Together \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id} and \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
774	upgrade to the corresponding free-sheaf coherences -- the free-pullback isomorphism at the
775	identity equals the component of the pullback-identity isomorphism, and likewise for the
776	composite -- by coproduct extensionality over the decomposition
777	\(\mathcal{O}^{\,\oplus I} = \bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\). These coherences discharge both
778	functoriality laws (identity and composition) of the Grassmannian
779	functor \cref{def:grassmannian_functor}.
780	
781	\begin{definition}
782	
783	\leanok
784	[The Grassmannian / Quot-of-trivial-sheaf functor \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\)]
785	  \label{def:grassmannian_functor}
786	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor}
787	  \uses{def:is_locally_free_of_rank, def:quot_functor, def:gr_rankQuotient,
788	    lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id, lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
789	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
790	  % Exercise (2) "Grassmannian as a Quot scheme" (read from
791	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L557-L564).
